prompt:
Tech-debt ticket, finally scheduled. apiclient.py is our Deskline helpdesk client and it's the classic god class: urllib calls, the X-Api-Key header, JSON encode/decode, the error-envelope mapping, and every ticket method all tangled in DesklineClient. Consequences we're living with: the mobile team wants transport+auth without the ticket methods and can't have it; nobody can unit-test a resource method without booting a mock server; and the last three bugfixes all collided in _call. Split it along the seams that are already visible. Stdlib only, no new deps.

Target layout -- a client/ package with three modules (plus its __init__.py):
- client/transport.py: `UrllibTransport(base_url, timeout=10)` with ONE job -- `request(method, path, headers=None, json_body=None)` returns `(status, body)` where body is the parsed JSON (None when the response is empty). It attaches Content-Type when it sends a JSON body, passes the caller's headers through verbatim, and has NO opinions about status codes: a 404 comes back as (404, envelope), it never raises for HTTP statuses. Socket-level trouble (refused, unreachable, timeout) raises `client.transport.TransportError`. It knows nothing about API keys.
- client/auth.py: `ApiKeyAuth(api_key)` with `headers()` returning a fresh dict {"X-Api-Key": key} each call -- fresh, because a shared dict some caller mutates is exactly the kind of spooky bug this refactor is supposed to end.
- client/resources.py: `TicketsApi(transport, auth)` -- any object with that transport request signature works, that injectability is the point -- with `get(ticket_id)`, `create(subject, body, priority="normal")`, `close(ticket_id, resolution)`, `list_open()` carrying the legacy semantics unchanged: 401 raises AuthError, other >=400 decodes the {"error": {"code", "message"}} envelope into ApiError(code, message, status), 404 on get returns None, and create's client-side validation (empty subject, unknown priority) raises ValueError before anything touches the transport. ApiError and AuthError (still a subclass) MOVE here; apiclient must re-export the very same class objects so every `except apiclient.ApiError:` in the fleet keeps catching errors raised by the new layers.

apiclient.DesklineClient itself becomes a thin facade composed from those layers -- same constructor `DesklineClient(base_url, api_key, timeout=10)`, same methods, same behavior. I cannot migrate four services this sprint, so the LEGACY BEHAVIOR block in test_client.py (which passes today) has to stay green the whole time. The one deliberate behavior change I am signing off on: connection-level failures surface as TransportError instead of leaking raw urllib exceptions. Nothing else observable may change.

test_client.py is the contract for both halves -- the legacy re-pins and the TARGET LAYOUT block that imports the new modules. `python3 test_client.py` printing ok means done. Don't touch the test file.
